text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy seaside village nestled among misty hills, there lived a fox named Freddy. Freddy loved his pinecone stash more than anything else. Every morning he’d scamper down to his favorite pinecone hill and fill his pockets.

One morning, something strange happened. Instead of dozens of shiny cones overflowing his cheeks, Freddy found himself carrying one odd, green thing—a strange ball perhaps? He tossed it aside suspiciously.



That afternoon on the village green, children gathered watching something extraordinary ripple through the water. Freddy darted closer suspiciously.

It glowed!

Freddy froze. Whoever knew stones could do that?!

Curiosity got the better of him. He scooped it up. It felt funny under his paw.

A girl passed by. Seeing Freddy’s weird new pet.)

"Oh my!"

Freddy gulped.

"I found it?"

"No!"

"Oh... kind?”

"Not really..."

That night under twinkling stars he dreamed of chasing ball-playing owls?

Tomorrow
